demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He has completely lost control — he's smashing the house apart, hitting us, and trying to break the front window to run away. (Call made by the parents; the boy is non-verbal right now.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A sudden loud construction noise next door set him off about two hours ago and we cannot calm him down or physically hold him — we are frightened he'll hurt himself on the glass or run into the road.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two hours ago, within moments of a loud unexpected construction noise starting next door
symptom_narrative.duration: unremitting for two hours and not settling
symptom_narrative.character: screaming and non-verbal noises, pacing, throwing and breaking things, hitting his parents, and repeatedly trying to smash the front window to get out
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parents say it is the worst they have ever seen and they cannot contain him
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: whole body — he is completely worked up and moving constantly
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it began, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have tried has worked; he will not put on his headphones and cannot be talked down
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the noise and being approached or touched seem to make it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he has some superficial scratches on his hands from throwing things; parents say he is breathing fast, looks flushed and sweaty
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has meltdowns but the parents say nothing this severe or this long before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he normally needs a lot of support to stay regulated; right now he is completely beyond their control

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a developmental / neurodevelopmental disorder — the parents describe long-standing difficulties with social communication, rigid routines and repetitive behaviours, and being very sensitive to noise and change
past_medical_history.how_managed: managed with a behaviour support team, environmental strategies and daily medication

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: attention and hyperactivity difficulties, and clumsiness / trouble with coordination
past_medical_history.how_managed: part of his usual developmental picture

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: a daily dose taken this morning

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: lorazepam kept at home for emergencies (PRN)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 12-year-old boy living with both parents who are his primary carers; parents present and made the call; they have been unable to physically contain him for two hours.

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: long-standing difficulties with social communication and reciprocal interaction, restricted and repetitive behaviours, sensory hypersensitivity to noise, attention/hyperactivity difficulties, and motor coordination difficulty; needs high-level carer support for emotional regulation

objective_data_offered.value: intensely agitated, pacing and moving constantly, screaming non-verbal sounds, throwing objects
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed over a chaotic video call; the carer cannot get close safely

objective_data_offered.value: he is breathing really fast and looks flushed and sweaty
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay observation by a distressed parent, not a measured value

objective_data_offered.value: several superficial scratches on the backs of his hands from throwing and hitting objects
living room badly damaged; a chair is wedged against the front door by the parents to stop him getting out; broken items on the floor
